pullRequest.title: [DOM-55102] - Create scaffolding for bootstrapping model-serving microservice

nodes.body:
These settings come from nucleus and their default values are defined there. They must not be defined on this file.

Here we will only add settings that are specific to the model-serving service and are not being inherited from nucleus.

Those settings will start with the `modelServing.` prefix instead of `com.cerebro.domino.`
@ddl-tnguyen unless I'm missing something I still think that settings that are retrieved from nucleus shouldn't be defined on the model-serving configuration file.

If those settings are not explicitly set by an admin in central config, they actually do not end up in the `Configuration` object we get from nucleus and will throw an error if you try to `.get` those values. 

In [these lines in Settings.scala](https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/blob/76ce145a07888e82633931e4ab3eca3c3de3c081/server/app/domino/server/lib/config/Settings.scala#L569C7-L574C8) nucleus checks `Configuration` for those optional values and otherwise sets them to the default.

My logic was that we should store those default values in the model-serving `application.conf` file, so that we can try to get the central config from the nucleus `Configuration` and otherwise fall back on the defaults defined in the model-serving `Configuration`. If an admin sets any of those values in central config, it will override the default. This is what `ModelServingConfigLoader.scala` ends up doing.
Change message to indicate that a `MongoCommandException` happened so that we can differentiate from the response on line 42.
Let's rename these last 4 settings to indicate that they are for model APIs. E.g. `defaultModelApihealthCheckPeriodSeconds`
This pattern for detecting missing keys is too verbose. I believe that `config.get[]()` will throw an exception that includes the missing key if not found.
These settings are OK to be in this file. They are needed to be able to get the configuration from nucleus.

Rename to `defaultModelApiHealthCheck` so that it's clearer that these are default values for the Model API entity, and not some generic health check settings. E.g. someone might thing that these are health check settings for the model-serving service.
We'll want to create settings for these 2 values instead of hardcoding them.
These keys must be retrieved from nucleus otherwise they can't be overridden via central config.

Configuration settings must be defined in nucleus otherwise they can't be overridden via central config.
The only settings on this file are the ones required to start the application so that it can get the configuration values from nucleus.
You forgot to remove these settings from this file, now that they've been added to `server/conf/application.conf`
